concept: Transfer / Move Matter, Energy, or Information Between Systems
description: To move something from a source to a recipient. Transfer is more specific than a general flow, and naming it does not by itself prove that the full amount arrived unchanged at the other end.
sound_symbolism: Hissing 'se' begins in continuous motion. The stress meets a firm velar closure in 'ka', the audible instant of crossing from one side to another. 'Ru' rolls free of that contact and settles into deep 'u', so the word approaches, touches, and departs along the path of a transfer.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb with the transferred item as object; a goal phrase precedes it: 'ha monaki wea ra monaki phialu sekaru.' (This component transfers water to that component.) The event-noun rule supplies transfer. Distinct from 'selu' (flow), 'kolua' (carry), 'sepho' (send), and 'wisola' (exchange).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Tracing transfers reveals where shared materials, energy, information, and burdens actually move through infrastructure.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Practical systems are understood by following substances and work from source through handoff to recipient.

tags.activity: directed movement from a source to a recipient
tags.physical: movement of matter or energy between components or systems
tags.spatial: a source-to-goal relation across a boundary
